name: one arm chin-up
instructions: Stand facing a pull-up bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Reach up and grab the bar with an underhand grip, with one hand gripping the bar and the other hand holding your wrist for support.
Hang from the bar with your arm fully extended, keeping your body straight and your core engaged.
Pull yourself up towards the bar by bending your elbow and squeezing your back muscles.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

Stand facing a pull-up bar with your feet shoulder-width apart. Reach up and grab the bar with an underhand grip, with one hand gripping the bar and the other hand holding your wrist for support. Hang from the bar with your arm fully extended, keeping your body straight and your core engaged. Pull yourself up towards the bar by bending your elbow and squeezing your back muscles. Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
